subject: English Language Arts

secondary_architecture_support.note: A condensed single-period adaptation is possible, but the two-day sequence remains primary because students need planning, checkpoint, and final writing stages.

materials_control_note: Planning materials and checkpoint materials remain teacher-controlled until the final response release point.

lesson_overview.overview: Students explore a familiar community or school issue across two class days, gather reasons and evidence in a task sheet, complete a teacher checkpoint, and then write a final argument paragraph in the declared final evidence location.
lesson_overview.essential_question: How can we use reasons and evidence to write a clear argument about a community issue?

sequence.focus: Introduce the issue, gather reasons and evidence, and prepare for the teacher checkpoint.
sequence.carryover: Student planning from the task sheet continues into Day 2.

sequence.focus: Re-open planning, refine reasons and evidence, and write the final argument paragraph in the final response sheet.
sequence.carryover: Day 1 planning stays available while students draft their final response.

integrity_checks: Teacher-only checkpoint guidance stays in teacher-facing outputs.
Student planning work stays in the task sheet until the final response release point.
Final evidence stays in the final response sheet, not in teacher materials or slide notes.

teacher_guide.big_idea: Argument writing becomes clearer when students gather reasons and evidence before drafting, use a checkpoint to refine their thinking, and keep final evidence in a declared student-facing artifact.
learning_goals: I can guide students through a two-day argument-writing sequence without collapsing planning and final evidence into the same artifact.
I can use a checkpoint to strengthen student reasoning before final writing.
I can keep final written evidence in the declared final response location.

timing.activity: Launch the community issue and clarify the purpose of the planning task sheet.
Students gather reasons and evidence in the task sheet.
Teacher uses the checkpoint sheet to confirm students are ready for final writing.
Students revisit Day 1 planning and strengthen reasons and evidence.
Students draft the final argument paragraph in the final response sheet.

teacher_notes: Do not release the final response sheet on Day 1.
Keep sentence starters and planning supports embedded in the student-facing artifacts.
Use the checkpoint to push students beyond opinion toward stronger reason-and-evidence connections.

slides.title: Writing About a Community Issue — Day 1

content.subtitle: Launch the issue, gather reasons and evidence, and get ready for the checkpoint.

content.scenario: A school is deciding whether to make lunch periods longer. Some students say it would improve focus and reduce stress. Others say it would create scheduling problems. How could we build a clear argument about this issue?
prompts: What opinion might someone have about this issue?
What reasons might support that opinion?
What evidence would make the argument stronger?

rows: Choose a clear opinion about the issue.
Record at least two reasons and supporting evidence in the task sheet.
Use the checkpoint to confirm you are ready for final writing tomorrow.

content.task: Use your task sheet to plan your argument so you can carry strong reasoning into Day 2.

task_sheet.title: Day 1 Task Sheet
instructions: Record reasons and evidence you can use again on Day 2.
Keep your planning clear enough that you can build a paragraph from it tomorrow.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: Write the community or school issue in your own words and state your opinion clearly.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: Record two reasons that support your opinion.

tasks.label: Part C
tasks.prompt: Add at least one piece of evidence or explanation for each reason.

tasks.label: Part D
tasks.prompt: List one question or weak spot you still need to improve before writing your paragraph.

embedded_supports: Sentence starter: One important reason is ___.
Box the reason you think is strongest before the checkpoint.
success_criteria: I collected reasons and evidence I can use tomorrow.
I kept my planning in the declared task-sheet location.

checkpoint_sheet.title: Day 1 Checkpoint Sheet
checkpoint_sheet.checkpoint_focus: Teacher checkpoint before Day 2 final response release.
look_fors: Student has at least two usable reasons.
Student can point to evidence or explanation for those reasons.
conference_prompts: Show me the strongest reason in your planning.
What evidence makes that reason believable?
What still needs strengthening before you draft tomorrow?
checkpoint_sheet.release_rule: Release the Day 2 final response sheet only when the student can point to usable reasons and evidence in the task sheet.

days.carryover_note: Use Day 1 planning before drafting the final argument paragraph.

slides.title: Writing About a Community Issue — Day 2

content.subtitle: Revisit your planning, strengthen your argument, and write your final paragraph.

content.scenario: Before you draft, look back at the reasons and evidence you carried from Day 1.
prompts: Which piece of evidence needs clearer explanation?
How will you organize your final paragraph?

rows: Review Day 1 planning before adding anything new.
Strengthen one reason or one piece of evidence in the task sheet.
Write the final argument paragraph only in the final response sheet.

prompts: Which reason made your argument strongest?
What changed between your planning and your final paragraph?
How did you keep your final evidence in the right place?

task_sheet.title: Day 2 Task Sheet
instructions: Re-read your Day 1 planning before making changes.
Strengthen one reason or one piece of evidence.
Keep this sheet as planning, not as the final evidence location.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: Review your strongest reason and explain why you are keeping it in your final paragraph.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: Revise one piece of evidence or explanation so it supports your opinion more clearly.

embedded_supports: Underline the reason you will lead with in your final paragraph.
Keep planning work separate from your final paragraph.
success_criteria: I improved the planning I started on Day 1.
I know which reasons and evidence belong in my final paragraph.
I kept planning separate from final evidence.

final_response_sheet.title: Day 2 Final Response Sheet
final_response_sheet.prompt: Write one argument paragraph that states your opinion clearly, gives reasons, and uses evidence or explanation to support your thinking.
planning_reminders: Use one or two strong reasons from your task sheet.
Explain why your evidence supports your argument.
Keep your final written answer on this sheet.
success_criteria: My paragraph states a clear opinion.
My paragraph uses reasons and supporting evidence.
My final evidence is recorded in the declared final response location.
